privileged_info_summary: The apply_source_cleanup task is a synthetic test case using Eclipse's Source > Clean Up feature on an InventoryManager Java project. The task setup creates 4 Java files with intentional code smells: Product.java and Category.java in src/com/warehouse/model/, InventoryService.java in src/com/warehouse/service/, and Main.java in src/com/warehouse/. Code quality issues include 9 unused imports (metadata lists only 3), 1 complete raw type declaration (List tags), 1 partial raw type match (Map inventory without the exact semicolon pattern), 2 string equality comparisons using '==' (metadata correctly identifies both), and 1 unnecessary boxing with Integer.valueOf(42). Three forbidden patterns in metadata do not exist in the generated code: 'Map inventory;', 'Set categories;', and 'Boolean.valueOf(true)'. One string comparison pattern 'name == inputName' is also not in the code. Methods requiring @Override annotations are marked with comments but not yet annotated.
